studyName: Discovering the Genetic Basis of Cleft Palate: CIDR

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes demographic data (n=3 variables; self-reported race, ethnicity, birth year), type of orofacial cleft and type abbreviation, syndromic cleft status, composite cleft status details (n=11 variables; composite cleft lip diagnosis, composite right cleft lip status, composite left cleft lips status, composite cleft alveolus status, composite right alveolus status, composite left alveolus status, composite submucous cleft palate diagnosis , composite cleft palate diagnosis, composite hard palate status, composite soft palate status, composite unknown palate status), maternal lifestyle information at different timepoints during pregnancy (n=12 variables; alcohol use, smoking, vitamin intake), and maternal disease status at different timepoints during pregnancy (n=7 variables; epilepsy, gestational diabetes).

tables.description: This pedigree data table contains family relationships in the format of family IDs, subject IDs, father IDs, mother IDs, and biological sex.
This sample attributes table includes the body site where sample was collected, analyte type, and tumor status.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent group information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
